name: Oil Industry Safety Directorate
oneLine: Cert CN mismatch (CN=www, apex unmatched); cert expires 2026-05-17; 0/6 hardening headers

tls.note: IMMINENT EXPIRY + CN/SAN mismatch (CN=www.oisd.gov.in but apex doesn't match)

emailAuth.note: SPF v=spf1 -all hard-fails everything; no DMARC recovery path

findings: Cert CN/SAN mismatch — CN=www.oisd.gov.in but apex unmatched
Broken SPF (-all rejects all senders, no DMARC recovery path)

urgentActions.what: Rotate TLS certificate before May 17 + correct CN/SAN to cover apex
Add HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy headers
Fix SPF: include actual mail infrastructure or set neutral if no mail sent from domain

fingerprints.stack: Apache server; apex 404; www subdomain serves
eolFlags: IMMINENT cert expiry; CN/SAN mismatch — apex returns browser warning
Apache 2.2.15 EOL since 2017 — 8+ years of unpatched CVEs

attackPaths.name: Cert expiry → phishing chain (19 days to impact)
attackPaths.entry: Cert expires 2026-05-17. CN/SAN mismatch already triggers warnings on apex. After expiry, all subdomains warn.
attackPaths.pivot: Users dismiss warnings (trained behaviour). Attacker MITMs on apex with rogue cert.
attackPaths.objective: Credential interception during a known-warning window.

attackPaths.name: Email spoofing via broken SPF (-all without senders) + absent DMARC
attackPaths.entry: SPF v=spf1 -all rejects all senders unless DMARC has recovery; DMARC absent.
attackPaths.pivot: Combined with absent DMARC, attacker spoofs @oisd.gov.in unimpeded.
attackPaths.objective: Phishing with trusted government safety entity branding.

attackPaths.name: Apache 2.2.15 EOL — unpatched RCE on connect subdomain
attackPaths.entry: Apache 2.2.15 EOL since 2017. Dozens of cumulative CVEs in mod_ssl, mod_proxy, mod_rewrite.
attackPaths.pivot: Endpoint returns 403 — restricted but reachable; if any auth path or known-CVE chain succeeds, RCE.
attackPaths.objective: Server compromise; lateral move to OISD internal network.

attackPaths.name: Subdomain enumeration → potential takeover
attackPaths.entry: CT logs reveal connect.oisd.gov.in and www.connect.oisd.gov.in.
attackPaths.pivot: If DNS points to deprovisioned cloud and entry not removed, subdomain takeover.
attackPaths.objective: Phishing with valid TLS via takeover.

paths.preAi: Cert expiry → browser warnings → phishing baseline
paths.mythos: AI-augmented attacker pre-stages rogue cert + DNS hijack timed to expiry minute

paths.preAi: Apache 2.2.15 EOL exposes unpatched RCE surface
paths.mythos: AI maps 8 years of CVEs against the live config and surfaces the most likely exploitable chain in minutes

cisoPatchList.title: EMERGENCY: Rotate TLS cert before 2026-05-17 with apex SAN coverage
cisoPatchList.fix: Renew cert. Ensure SAN includes BOTH apex (oisd.gov.in) AND www. Re-issue if current cert only covers www. Validate apex serves 200 OK post-renewal.
cisoPatchList.validation: openssl s_client | x509 -noout -ext subjectAltName shows both apex and www

cisoPatchList.title: Fix broken SPF — add real includes or set neutral
cisoPatchList.fix: Replace 'v=spf1 -all' with 'v=spf1 include:<actual mail provider> -all'. If domain doesn't send mail, document and keep -all but pair with DMARC p=reject for clarity.
cisoPatchList.validation: Legitimate mail from approved senders delivers; spoofed mail bounces

cisoPatchList.title: Publish DMARC record — start p=quarantine, escalate to p=reject
cisoPatchList.fix: DMARC: 'v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; rua=mailto:dmarc@oisd.gov.in; fo=1'. Monitor 2 weeks; escalate to p=reject.

cisoPatchList.title: Apache 2.2.15 — upgrade to 2.4.62+ or migrate to nginx reverse proxy
cisoPatchList.fix: (a) In-place upgrade to Apache 2.4.62+ with regression testing OR (b) Deploy nginx 1.26+ in reverse-proxy mode pointing to hardened backend. Test connect endpoint thoroughly.
cisoPatchList.validation: curl -sI connect.oisd.gov.in | grep Server shows Apache 2.4.62+ or nginx

cisoPatchList.title: Harden CSP — remove unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval
Add the missing 6 hardening headers on apex
cisoPatchList.fix: HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy.

cisoPatchList.title: Subdomain inventory + takeover prevention
cisoPatchList.fix: For each CT-logged subdomain, verify DNS points to owned infra. Remove dangling CNAMEs. Implement CT monitoring.
cisoPatchList.validation: Official subdomain register; monthly CT cross-check

cisoPatchList.title: Reverse proxy / WAF deployment
cisoPatchList.fix: ModSecurity OWASP CRS or Cloudflare. Block traversal, SQLi, mod_rewrite exploit patterns.
cisoPatchList.validation: WAF testing: CVE PoC payloads return 403

cisoPatchList.title: Apache deprecation roadmap for entire OISD estate
cisoPatchList.fix: Document timeline to retire all Apache 2.2.x and migrate to supported versions.
cisoPatchList.validation: Migration plan filed with target dates

phase2.headlineQuestion: OISD is fragile across three dimensions — imminent cert expiry (19 days), broken email authentication, and Apache 2.2.15 EOL with 8+ years of unpatched CVEs. What is the incident response if cert renewal fails on 2026-05-17?
